pattern: Interval dynamic programming that extends a palindromic subsequence inward whenever the boundary characters match.
hints: A single character is a palindrome of length one, which seeds the diagonal of your table.
For a range, if the two end characters are equal they both join the best palindrome of the inner range, adding two.
If the ends differ, drop one end or the other and take the better of the two shorter ranges, filling the table by increasing range length.

edge_inputs.description: no repeats so answer is one

stress.description: random small-alphabet strings to force long palindromic subsequences
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    alpha = 'abc'
    n = rng.randrange(1, max(2, size) + 1)
    return [''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(n))]
